title: Delicate Venezuelan Blackcurrant Empanadas
description: A cultured and festive Venezuelan dish featuring blackcurrant jam and a shio-koji cure, perfect for special occasions, and can be adapted to be sugar-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, shio-koji, and salt.
Add the melted butter, beaten egg, and cold water to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of blackcurrant jam in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the jam to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
